title: Mastery Exam: Overflow, Geometry, and Effects
summary: Mastery Exam: Overflow, Geometry, and Effects retrieves prior skills in an original independent multi-effect production incident scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css overflow, css sizing constraints, css transforms, css filters, css shadows depth, css containment effects, css effects debugging in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real independent multi-effect production incident artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Community Needs Survey geometry and effects work here. -->
starterFiles.css: /* Continue the overflow, geometry, and effects system here. */

steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Reconstruct the verification workflow. Scenario: independent multi-effect production incident.
steps.why: Mastery Exam: Overflow, Geometry, and Effects makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 1 in independent multi-effect production incident.

content.text: Order the independent multi-effect production incident work by dependency and verify that each phase creates evidence needed by the next.

hints: Mastery Exam: Overflow, Geometry, and Effects checkpoint 1: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Mastery Exam: Overflow, Geometry, and Effects checkpoint 1: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: Overflow, Geometry, and Effects checkpoint 1: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: independent multi-effect production incident phase 1.1: locate the exact overflowing box and content
independent multi-effect production incident phase 1.2: inspect intrinsic size min size wrapping and containing block
independent multi-effect production incident phase 1.3: repair the constraint or choose an intentional scroll or clipping contract
independent multi-effect production incident phase 1.4: test long text zoom focus touch and keyboard scrolling

options.text: Independent multi-effect production incident case 2: fixed height is the most reliable way to keep cards aligned.
Independent multi-effect production incident case 2: use intrinsic sizing and min or max constraints, allow content-driven block growth, and test extreme content before adding fixed dimensions.
Independent multi-effect production incident case 2: use max-content for every narrow-screen control because intrinsic width always fits the viewport.

options.text: Independent multi-effect production incident case 3: transforms move surrounding layout boxes and create space for neighbors.

Independent multi-effect production incident case 3: calculate original and painted geometry separately, compose transforms in a known order, and reserve layout space without relying on transformed bounds.
Independent multi-effect production incident case 3: translate a card downward to make following content move down with it.

options.text: Independent multi-effect production incident case 4: a brightness filter reliably repairs contrast for arbitrary images and text.

Independent multi-effect production incident case 4: use filters only for bounded visual treatment, preserve an unfiltered fallback, and remeasure contrast, legibility, and performance on actual content.
Independent multi-effect production incident case 4: hue-rotate every status icon and assume users understand the new state colors.

options.text: Independent multi-effect production incident case 5: more shadows always make interactive elevation clearer.
Independent multi-effect production incident case 5: use restrained shadows as secondary presentation and keep boundaries, hierarchy, and focus understandable with shadows removed.
Independent multi-effect production incident case 5: use a shadow as the only focus indicator because it looks softer than an outline.

options.text: Independent multi-effect production incident case 7: raising z-index repeatedly is a dependable stacking-context fix.
Independent multi-effect production incident case 7: reduce the case, toggle one context-creating or clipping property at a time, record computed evidence, and repair the first causal boundary.
Independent multi-effect production incident case 7: rewrite all component CSS because isolated property toggles cannot represent production.

options.text: Independent multi-effect production incident case 8: overflow hidden is a harmless way to repair any layout that extends beyond a card.

Independent multi-effect production incident case 8: identify the overflowing content and sizing cause, preserve access, and use auto, scroll, clip, hidden, or visible only for an intentional contract.
Independent multi-effect production incident case 8: use overflow clip on every focusable card because clipped focus is still keyboard reachable.

options.text: Independent multi-effect production incident case 27: z-index values compare globally across every element on the page.

Independent multi-effect production incident case 27: draw the stacking-context tree and containing blocks, compare sibling contexts first, and change the boundary rather than escalating descendant z-index.
Independent multi-effect production incident case 27: set the trapped child to z-index 999999 because large values escape ancestor contexts.

options.text: Independent multi-effect production incident case 28: raising z-index repeatedly is a dependable stacking-context fix.
Independent multi-effect production incident case 28: reduce the case, toggle one context-creating or clipping property at a time, record computed evidence, and repair the first causal boundary.
Independent multi-effect production incident case 28: keep adding overflow hidden and z-index until the screenshot matches.

options.text: Independent multi-effect production incident case 29: overflow hidden is a harmless way to repair any layout that extends beyond a card.

Independent multi-effect production incident case 29: identify the overflowing content and sizing cause, preserve access, and use auto, scroll, clip, hidden, or visible only for an intentional contract.
Independent multi-effect production incident case 29: apply overflow hidden to the body so no component can cause horizontal scrolling.

options.text: Independent multi-effect production incident case 30: fixed height is the most reliable way to keep cards aligned.
Independent multi-effect production incident case 30: use intrinsic sizing and min or max constraints, allow content-driven block growth, and test extreme content before adding fixed dimensions.
Independent multi-effect production incident case 30: use max-content for every narrow-screen control because intrinsic width always fits the viewport.
